description: A senior bio-informatics researcher has asked you to set up a **repeatable one-way sync workflow** between her “work-in-progress” data on the LOCAL workstation and an archive folder that she mounts at /home/user/remote/mirror/data (it represents the “remote server” in this exercise). INITIAL STATE (already present): 1. LOCAL source root: /home/user/datasets/raw_data ├── exp1 │ ├── results.csv (plain UTF-8 text) │ ├── notes.txt (plain UTF-8 text) │ └── .temp │ └── cache.tmp (binary garbage) └── exp2 ├── data_part1.csv (plain UTF-8 text) ├── data_part2.csv (plain UTF-8 text) └── .ignore (0-byte placeholder) 2. DESTINATION root: /home/user/remote/mirror/data – the directory exists but is EMPTY. REQUIREMENTS – do this **using 5–10 shell commands** in total: a. Synchronise ONLY the “valuable” files: every file whose name ends in “.csv” (case-sensitive) found under /home/user/datasets/raw_data. • Sub-directories must be recreated exactly. • Hidden files/directories (names that start with “.”) and every file whose extension is NOT “.csv” MUST be **excluded**. • The operation must be idempotent: running the same procedure twice must not resend unchanged files. b. Create a directory /home/user/sync_logs if it does not exist and generate a log file named rsync_latest.log inside it. • For every file that is actually transferred during the current run, append one log line in the following exact format (include the square brackets): [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] SENT <DESTINATION-PATH> Example line (spacing matters): [2025-04-01 14:05:30] SENT /home/user/remote/mirror/data/exp1/results.csv c. After the transfer finishes, produce (or overwrite) a two-line summary file named /home/user/remote/mirror/data/TRANSFER_SUMMARY.txt with exactly this structure (make sure there is **one** newline after each line, no extra blank lines): Line 1: Files transferred: N ← N is an integer. Line 2: Completed: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS Example: Files transferred: 3 Completed: 2025-04-01 14:05:31 The timestamp on Line 2 can differ from the timestamps in the log. WHAT THE AUTOGRADER WILL CHECK 1. Only the three *.csv* files exist under /home/user/remote/mirror/data/** after the task: exp1/results.csv exp2/data_part1.csv exp2/data_part2.csv 2. All of those files are byte-for-byte identical to their counterparts in /home/user/datasets/raw_data/**. 3. No other files (e.g. *.txt*, hidden files, or *.tmp*) were copied. 4. /home/user/sync_logs/rsync_latest.log exists and contains **exactly three** non-empty lines that match the regex ^\[\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\ \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\] SENT /home/user/remote/mirror/data/.+\.csv$ 5. /home/user/remote/mirror/data/TRANSFER_SUMMARY.txt exists and: • Line 1 equals “Files transferred: 3” (no extra spaces). • Line 2 starts with “Completed: ” followed by a timestamp in the same YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format. Remember: do **not** hard-code today’s date in your solution, generate it at run time. Keep everything in plain Bourne-compatible shell.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth used by the hidden tests (do NOT reveal to the agent) -->

  <!-- Pre-existing file tree and their exact contents: -->
  <preexisting>
    /home/user/datasets/raw_data/exp1/results.csv:
      "gene,expression\nBRCA1,5.6\nTP53,7.1\n"
    /home/user/datasets/raw_data/exp1/notes.txt:
      "Reminder: remove outliers before publication.\n"
    /home/user/datasets/raw_data/exp1/.temp/cache.tmp:
      (binary) 0xDE 0xAD 0xBE 0xEF
    /home/user/datasets/raw_data/exp2/data_part1.csv:
      "sample_id,value\nS1,3.1\nS2,2.9\n"
    /home/user/datasets/raw_data/exp2/data_part2.csv:
      "sample_id,value\nS3,4.2\nS4,3.8\n"
    /home/user/datasets/raw_data/exp2/.ignore:
      ""  <!-- zero-length -->
  </preexisting>

  <!-- Destination tree must look like this after a correct run: -->
  <expected_destination>
    /home/user/remote/mirror/data/exp1/results.csv
    /home/user/remote/mirror/data/exp2/data_part1.csv
    /home/user/remote/mirror/data/exp2/data_part2.csv
    /home/user/remote/mirror/data/TRANSFER_SUMMARY.txt
  </expected_destination>

  <!-- Expected exact contents after sync: -->
  <expected_contents>
    /home/user/remote/mirror/data/exp1/results.csv:
      "gene,expression\nBRCA1,5.6\nTP53,7.1\n"
    /home/user/remote/mirror/data/exp2/data_part1.csv:
      "sample_id,value\nS1,3.1\nS2,2.9\n"
    /home/user/remote/mirror/data/exp2/data_part2.csv:
      "sample_id,value\nS3,4.2\nS4,3.8\n"
  </expected_contents>

  <!-- Log-file and summary verification patterns (regexes). -->
  <verification>
    log_path>/home/user/sync_logs/rsync_latest.log</log_path>
    log_line_regex>^\[\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\ \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\] SENT /home/user/remote/mirror/data/.+\.csv$</log_line_regex>

    summary_path>/home/user/remote/mirror/data/TRANSFER_SUMMARY.txt</summary_path>
    summary_first_line>Files transferred: 3</summary_first_line>
    summary_second_line_regex>^Completed: \d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}$</summary_second_line_regex>
  </verification>
